title: Khalid ibn al-Walid — the Sword of God

intro.title: From Iraq to the Yarmuk
intro.body: Khalid ibn al-Walid won twin campaigns under Abu Bakr and Umar — first the Sasanid border in Iraq, then a 600-mile desert ride across the Syrian Hamad to reinforce the army in Palestine. The Yarmuk battle in August 636 ended Byzantine Syria.

stops.name: al-Hira
stops.note: Lakhmid capital, capitulates spring 633. Khalid's base for the Iraqi campaign.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Lakeside Arab Christian town, palm groves, Khalid's bedouin cavalry pitching tents outside the wall.

stops.name: Anbar
stops.note: Euphrates city captured June 633. The 'Action of the Eye' — Khalid's archers shoot defenders at the loopholes (Tabari I.2061).
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Mud-brick walls along the Euphrates, archers in close ranks at the base of the wall.

stops.name: Quraqir
stops.note: Last reliable well before the waterless Syrian Hamad. Khalid waters camels here in May 634 for the famous five-day desert crossing.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Cluster of black tents around a single deep well, camels being filled with water, sun overhead.

stops.name: Damascus
stops.note: Captured 18 September 635 after a six-month siege. Khalid takes the East Gate at dawn while Abu Ubayda accepts capitulation at another.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Walled garden city in the Ghouta, dawn light on the East Gate, Arab cavalry inside.

stops.name: Yarmuk
stops.note: 15-20 August 636. Six-day battle on the Yarmuk gorge. Vahan's Byzantine army shatters and Syria is open to the Caliphate.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Dust-storm battle on a high plateau ending at the lip of a deep gorge, broken Byzantine standards.
